topics: Overview of material types: metals, polymers, composites, and ceramics. Criteria for material selection in the automotive industry
Ferrous and non-ferrous alloys: applications of steel, aluminium, magnesium, and titanium alloys
Polymers and composites: thermoplastics, thermosetting plastics, elastomers, reinforced plastics, and carbon fibre composites
Ceramics and glass: structural ceramics, advanced ceramics, and glass materials for automotive applications
Importance of weight reduction in automotive design
Advanced lightweight materials: advanced high-strength steels (AHSS), aluminium alloys, and magnesium alloys
High-performance materials: superalloys, titanium alloys, and their applications in high-performance vehicles
Smart materials and shape memory alloys
Surface engineering and coatings: surface treatments, coatings for corrosion resistance and wear protection
Additive manufacturing: overview of processes and their applications in the automotive industry
Nanomaterials: properties, synthesis, and applications
Life cycle assessment (LCA) of automotive materials
Impact of materials on vehicle emissions and fuel efficiency
Recycling strategies for metals, polymers, and composites
End-of-life vehicle (ELV) recycling and circular economy concepts
Bio-based and biodegradable materials
Eco-design principles and their implementation in automotive manufacturing
Environmental regulations and sustainability standards for the automotive industry
Requirements for lightweighting and energy efficiency in electric vehicles (EVs)
Battery materials: lithium-ion, solid-state batteries, and future alternatives
Advanced materials for fuel efficiency: aerodynamics and lightweight materials
Use of composites and advanced alloys in structural components
Smart and functional materials: self-healing materials, sensors, and conductive materials
Integration of smart materials in vehicle systems and components
Future directions in automotive materials: trends in innovation, sustainability, and manufacturing
Research and development opportunities in new materials and technologies
